title: Olive Oil Cake with Brown Butter Frosting
ingredients: 1 ½ cups extra virgin olive oil
1 ¼ cups whole milk, room temperature
Zest and juice of 1 large lemon
Zest of 2 medium or large oranges
instructions: Prepare a 9-inch springform pan by lightly greasing with olive oil, butter, or spray oil, and line the bottom with a round cut of parchment paper.
Beat together sugar and olive oil until combined.
Add eggs one at a time to the sugar and oil mixture, mixing between each egg.
Add lemon juice and zest to the batter and mix.
Add milk and mix until incorporated.
Mix all dry ingredients (flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda) and sift together.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture and mix until incorporated, being careful not to over-mix.
Pour batter into the prepared springform pan.
Place the springform pan onto a cookie sheet (it may leak while baking).
Bake at 325°F for 80 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean with a few crumbs.
While the cake cools, make the frosting: melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
Stir the butter frequently and scrape the bottom of the pan until brown specks appear and the aroma becomes nutty and toasty.
Remove the browned butter from heat and let cool completely.
Beat the cooled brown butter and powdered sugar together.
Add vanilla, salt, and cream, mixing until smooth.
If the frosting is too thick, add more cream or milk as needed.
Frost the sides and top of the cake (or just the top if preferred).
Garnish with orange slices and rosemary sprigs.
notes: Inspired by Tulie Bakery in Salt Lake City
Use room temperature eggs and milk for best results
Be careful not to over-mix the batter to maintain a tender crumb
The springform pan may leak during baking, so placing it on a cookie sheet is recommended
All ingredients should be cooled completely before frosting the cake
The brown butter frosting should be beaten until the butter is completely cool to avoid melting
Optional garnish with orange slices and fresh rosemary sprigs
